devanagari: अरिष्टासुरवधः कंसस्य अक्रूरं प्रति नन्दगोकुल गमनायादेशश्च । श्री शुक उवाच । अथ तर्ह्यागतो गोष्ठं अरिष्टो वृषभासुरः । महीं महाककुत्कायः ...
english_devnagari: ariṣṭāsuravadhaḥ kaṃsasya akrūraṃ prati nandagokula gamanāyādeśaścaśrī śuka uvācaatha tarhyāgato goṣṭhaṃ ariṣṭo vṛṣabhāsuraḥmahīṃ mahākakutkāyaḥ kampayan khuravikṣatām
translation: Oh highly intelligent superintendent (or keeper) of my elephants! Listen to this command of mine. Our Elephant, Kuvalayāpīḍa has the rut flowing from its temples.

devanagari: रम्भमाणः खरतरं पदा च विलिखन् महीम् । उद्यम्य पुच्छं वप्राणि विषाणाग्रेण चोद्धरन् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: rambhamāṇaḥ kharataraṃ padā ca vilikhan mahīmudyamya pucchaṃ vaprāṇi viṣāṇāgreṇa coddharan
translation: It should be placed at the entrance of the arena with a heavy iron bar in its trunk. The two sons of Vasudeva (Ānakadundubhi) are to be killed.

devanagari: किञ्चित्किञ्चित् शकृन् मुञ्चन् मूत्रयन् स्तब्धलोचनः । यस्य निर्ह्रादितेनाङ्ग निष्ठुरेण गवां नृणाम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kiñcitkiñcit śakṛn muñcan mūtrayan stabdhalocanaḥyasya nirhrāditenāṅga niṣṭhureṇa gavāṃ nṛṇām
translation: But even the conductor of the elephant got agitated in mind and submitted, “How can I kill the two sons of the sister of His Majesty, the Lord of the earth?”

devanagari: पतन्त्यकालतो गर्भाः स्रवन्ति स्म भयेन वै । निर्विशन्ति घना यस्य ककुद्यचलशङ्कया ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: patantyakālato garbhāḥ sravanti sma bhayena vainirviśanti ghanā yasya kakudyacalaśaṅkayā
translation: When addressed thus by the elephant-driver, the wicked-minded Kaṃsa ordered him again, making all the assembled persons hear it for compliance.

devanagari: तं तीक्ष्णशृङ्गमुद्वीक्ष्य गोप्यो गोपाश्च तत्रसुः । पशवो दुद्रुवुर्भीता राजन् संत्यज्य गोकुलम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tīkṣṇaśṛṅgamudvīkṣya gopyo gopāśca tatrasuḥpaśavo dudruvurbhītā rājan saṃtyajya gokulam
translation:
Kaṃsa said:
Carefully listen to this command personally given by me. You should all hear it and bear it in mind. Listen to the reason why from the start, I have been a sworn enemy of these, even though they are my relatives.

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्णेति ते सर्वे गोविन्दं शरणं ययुः । भगवानपि तद्वीक्ष्य गोकुलं भयविद्रुतम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇeti te sarve govindaṃ śaraṇaṃ yayuḥbhagavānapi tadvīkṣya gokulaṃ bhayavidrutam
translation: On one occasion, my highly fortunate lotus-eyed mother who was in her youth, was wandering about in the garden of the palace, when a gentle cool breeze was blowing.

devanagari: मा भैष्टेति गिराश्वास्य वृषासुरमुपाह्वयत् । गोपालैः पशुभिर्मन्द त्रासितैः किमसत्तम ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: mā bhaiṣṭeti girāśvāsya vṛṣāsuramupāhvayatgopālaiḥ paśubhirmanda trāsitaiḥ kimasattama
translation: It was the spring time when the garden was beautified with full-blown Aśoka, Bakul, areca nuts and Punnāga trees, with humming and hovering swarms of black bees, resounded with the sweet cooings of cuckoos. It was attractive with the group of dancing peacocks and the herds of playful monkeys; it was lined with rows of Sāla trees.

devanagari: बलदर्पहाहं दुष्टानां त्वद्विधानां दुरात्मनाम् । इत्यास्फोट्याच्युतोऽरिष्टं तलशब्देन कोपयन् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: baladarpahāhaṃ duṣṭānāṃ tvadvidhānāṃ durātmanāmityāsphoṭyācyuto'riṣṭaṃ talaśabdena kopayan
translation: It was the spring time when the garden was beautified with full-blown Aśoka, Bakul, areca nuts and Punnāga trees, with humming and hovering swarms of black bees, resounded with the sweet cooings of cuckoos. It was attractive with the group of dancing peacocks and the herds of playful monkeys; it was lined with rows of Sāla trees.

devanagari: सख्युरंसे भुजाभोगं प्रसार्यावस्थितो हरिः । सोऽप्येवं कोपितोऽरिष्टः खुरेणावनिमुल्लिखन् । उद्यत्पुच्छभ्रमन्मेघः क्रुद्धः कृष्णमुपाद्रवत् ...
english_devnagari: sakhyuraṃse bhujābhogaṃ prasāryāvasthito hariḥso'pyevaṃ kopito'riṣṭaḥ khureṇāvanimullikhanudyatpucchabhramanmeghaḥ kruddhaḥ kṛṣṇamupādravat
translation: While my mother of auspicious vows was sauntering there, she, who was well adorned and had taken her bath after menstruation, and was agitated with the passion of love, contemplated of her husband.

devanagari: अग्रन्यस्तविषाणाग्रः स्तब्धासृग् लोचनोऽच्युतम् । कटाक्षिप्याद्रवत् तूर्ण् इन्द्रमुक्तोऽशनिर्यथा ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: agranyastaviṣāṇāgraḥ stabdhāsṛg locano'cyutamkaṭākṣipyādravat tūrṇ indramukto'śaniryathā
translation: At that time a certain Gandharva, Drumila by name, was going through the garden, unseen by others. He noticed that she was alone and ready for the occasion. He being able to read the thoughts and intentions in the minds of all, understood what she was thinking about. He assumed the form of Ugrasena and resembled him in speech and smiles.

devanagari: गृहीत्वा शृङ्गयोस्तं वा अष्टादश पदानि सः । प्रत्यपोवाह भगवान् गजः प्रतिगजं यथा ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhītvā śṛṅgayostaṃ vā aṣṭādaśa padāni saḥpratyapovāha bhagavān gajaḥ pratigajaṃ yathā
translation: At that time a certain Gandharva, Drumila by name, was going through the garden, unseen by others. He noticed that she was alone and ready for the occasion. He being able to read the thoughts and intentions in the minds of all, understood what she was thinking about. He assumed the form of Ugrasena and resembled him in speech and smiles.

devanagari: सोऽपविद्धो भगवता पुनरुत्थाय सत्वरम् । आपतत् स्विन्नसर्वाङ्गो निःश्वसन् क्रोधमूर्च्छितः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: so'paviddho bhagavatā punarutthāya satvaramāpatat svinnasarvāṅgo niḥśvasan krodhamūrcchitaḥ
translation: The Gandharva who entertained a lustful passion about that chaste lady and was love-striken, got her consent. Being perfectly ignorant about the reality, she accompanied and had a pleasant time with him in that forest grove.

devanagari: तं आपतन्तं स निगृह्य शृङ्गयोः । पदा समाक्रम्य निपात्य भूतले । निष्पीडयामास यथाऽर्द्रमम्बरं । कृत्वा विषाणेन जघान सोऽपतत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ āpatantaṃ sa nigṛhya śṛṅgayoḥpadā samākramya nipātya bhūtaleniṣpīḍayāmāsa yathā'rdramambaraṃkṛtvā viṣāṇena jaghāna so'patat
translation: That chaste lady somehow came to discover him to be somebody else during the act of intercourse. She was extremely distressed at heart and with her mind and eyes agitated demanded, “Who are you, Oh wretched cheat? That a wicked person like you has perpetrated this deed condemned by the noble?”

devanagari: असृग् वमन् मूत्रशकृत् समुत्सृजन् । क्षिपंश्च पादाननवस्थितेक्षणः । जगाम कृच्छ्रं निर्ऋतेरथ क्षयं । पुष्पैः किरन्तो हरिमीडिरे सुराः ॥ १४...
english_devnagari: asṛg vaman mūtraśakṛt samutsṛjankṣipaṃśca pādānanavasthitekṣaṇaḥjagāma kṛcchraṃ nirṛteratha kṣayaṃpuṣpaiḥ kiranto harimīḍire surāḥ
translation: The long-armed Drumila tried to console her who demanded this of him. He gently explained, “Oh good lady! We belong to a family of Gandharvas. We are equal to the celestials and are there beloved attendants.

devanagari: एवं कुकुद्मिनं हत्वा स्तूयमानः द्विजातिभिः । विवेश गोष्ठं सबलो गोपीनां नयनोत्सवः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kukudminaṃ hatvā stūyamānaḥ dvijātibhiḥviveśa goṣṭhaṃ sabalo gopīnāṃ nayanotsavaḥ
translation: Oh auspicious lady! Please note that we (Gandharvas) are not easily accessible to females of the human species like you who are fit to be enjoyed by human beings only. And there is a lot of difference between men who are subject to birth, death and such other physical and mental ailments and the celestials (who are unaffected by them), Oh pretty woman!”

devanagari: अरिष्टे निहते दैत्ये कृष्णेनाद्‍भुतकर्मणा । कंसायाथाह भगवान् नारदो देवदर्शनः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ariṣṭe nihate daitye kṛṣṇenād‍bhutakarmaṇākaṃsāyāthāha bhagavān nārado devadarśanaḥ
translation: While he was talking thus, the lady with her mind overwhelmed with rage, interrupted, “Do you want to appease me now after perpetrating that nefarious act?

devanagari: यशोदायाः सुतां कन्यां देवक्याः कृष्णमेव च । रामं च रोहिणीपुत्रं वसुदेवेन बिभ्यता ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśodāyāḥ sutāṃ kanyāṃ devakyāḥ kṛṣṇameva carāmaṃ ca rohiṇīputraṃ vasudevena bibhyatā
translation: In defiance of the deities presiding over water, fire, earth, air, the ether, the moon, the sun, and disregarding the presiding divinities of the cardinal points, Time- Spirit, the two twilights (the dawn and the dusk) and the Dharma itself, you have adulterated with me.

devanagari: न्यस्तौ स्वमित्रे नन्दे वै याभ्यां ते पुरुषा हताः । निशम्य तद्‍भोजपतिः कोपात् प्रचलितेन्द्रियः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: nyastau svamitre nande vai yābhyāṃ te puruṣā hatāḥniśamya tad‍bhojapatiḥ kopāt pracalitendriyaḥ
translation: Like unto a restraint-less elephant emitting ichor and blind with passion, you, wretched fellow, have made me, who was like a pure, translucent lotus-pond in a forest, turbid and impure. You wrethed-most vile immortal! Do you again dare to try to conciliate me?”

devanagari: निशातमसिमादत्त वसुदेवजिघांसया । निवारितो नारदेन तत्सुतौ मृत्युमात्मनः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: niśātamasimādatta vasudevajighāṃsayānivārito nāradena tatsutau mṛtyumātmanaḥ
translation: While she was censuring him thus, he, being afraid of being imprecated, urged through fear, “Oh Lotuseyed lady! Sexual relation between immortals and women is of the regular order while that between men and celestial women is of the irregular order and is unnatural.

devanagari: ज्ञात्वा लोहमयैः पाशैः बबन्ध सह भार्यया । प्रतियाते तु देवर्षौ कंस आभाष्य केशिनम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: jñātvā lohamayaiḥ pāśaiḥ babandha saha bhāryayāpratiyāte tu devarṣau kaṃsa ābhāṣya keśinam
translation: While she was censuring him thus, he, being afraid of being imprecated, urged through fear, “Oh Lotuseyed lady! Sexual relation between immortals and women is of the regular order while that between men and celestial women is of the irregular order and is unnatural.

devanagari: प्रेषयामास हन्येतां भवता रामकेशवौ । ततो मुष्टिकचाणूर शलतोशलकादिकान् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: preṣayāmāsa hanyetāṃ bhavatā rāmakeśavautato muṣṭikacāṇūra śalatośalakādikān
translation: Hence, Oh lady with lotus fragrance, please note that I have not committed an offence. My semen shall not go waste. A son will be born to you.

devanagari: अमात्यान् हस्तिपांश्चैव समाहूयाह भोजराट् । भो भो निशम्यतामेतद् वीरचाणूरमुष्टिकौ ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: amātyān hastipāṃścaiva samāhūyāha bhojarāṭbho bho niśamyatāmetad vīracāṇūramuṣṭikau
translation: He will be affluent, intelligent, highly energetic and daring. My words shall not prove vain and untrue.” While he was speaking thus, my enraged mother who was deeply distressed at the loss of her irrecoverable chastity, exploded in wrath: “You have transgressed the bounds of dharma and have taken to different (sinful) course of conduct, and are a wicked sinner, a follower of the wrong (immoral) path, your son shall be cruel, characterised by criminality and void of good qualities. He will never receive the blessings of gods, Brāhmaṇas and sages”.

devanagari: नन्दव्रजे किलासाते सुतावानकदुन्दुभेः । रामकृष्णौ ततो मह्यं मृत्युः किल निदर्शितः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: nandavraje kilāsāte sutāvānakadundubheḥrāmakṛṣṇau tato mahyaṃ mṛtyuḥ kila nidarśitaḥ
translation: He will be affluent, intelligent, highly energetic and daring. My words shall not prove vain and untrue.” While he was speaking thus, my enraged mother who was deeply distressed at the loss of her irrecoverable chastity, exploded in wrath: “You have transgressed the bounds of dharma and have taken to different (sinful) course of conduct, and are a wicked sinner, a follower of the wrong (immoral) path, your son shall be cruel, characterised by criminality and void of good qualities. He will never receive the blessings of gods, Brāhmaṇas and sages”.

devanagari: भवद्‍भ्यामिह सम्प्राप्तौ हन्येतां मल्ललीलया । मञ्चाः क्रियन्तां विविधा मल्लरङ्गपरिश्रिताः । पौरा जानपदाः सर्वे पश्यन्तु स्वैरसंयुगम् ॥...
english_devnagari: bhavad‍bhyāmiha samprāptau hanyetāṃ mallalīlayāmañcāḥ kriyantāṃ vividhā mallaraṅgapariśritāḥpaurā jānapadāḥ sarve paśyantu svairasaṃyugam
translation: [1] Let Vasudeva, Ugrasena, that Nanda and Devaka, the cowherds and Yādavas and all of their followers and partisans witness with tearful eyes the killing of their own sons. Let all the people in the country be invited and let them witness the free style wrestling[2] (svaira-saṃ-yugam).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Vṛndāvana edition gives these 33 additional verses.

Footnote [2]:
but pauruṣam [pauruṣa]— ‘my prowess’ interprets Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: महामात्र त्वया भद्र रङ्गद्वार्युपनीयताम् । द्विपः कुवलयापीडो जहि तेन ममाहितौ ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: mahāmātra tvayā bhadra raṅgadvāryupanīyatāmdvipaḥ kuvalayāpīḍo jahi tena mamāhitau
translation: Oh conductor of my elephant! You are a good man: The elephant Kuvalayāpīḍa is to be posted by you at the entrance of the arena. See to it that my two enemies (Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) are killed by it.

devanagari: आरभ्यतां धनुर्यागः चतुर्दश्यां यथाविधि । विशसन्तु पशून् मेध्यान् भूतराजाय मीढुषे ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: ārabhyatāṃ dhanuryāgaḥ caturdaśyāṃ yathāvidhiviśasantu paśūn medhyān bhūtarājāya mīḍhuṣe
translation: Let the sacrifice connected with the bow (dhanuryāga)[3] be commenced with due formality on the fourteenth day (of the dark half[4]). Let the priests offer sacrificial animals as victims to the king of bhūtas (goblins) i.e. god Śiva who is the bestower of all boons (to god Śiva the bestower of boons and the ruler over all beings).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Subodhinī explains: As against the Viṣṇu-power of Kṛṣṇa, Kaṃsa wanted to counter-balance it by propitiating god Śiva. The worship of god Śiva as presiding over the bow (which would thereby be victorious in fighting) is called dhanuryāga in Śiva-tantra.

Footnote [4]:
Subodhinī states it to be the Śivarātrī day.

devanagari: इत्याज्ञाप्यार्थतन्त्रज्ञ आहूय यदुपुङ्गवम् । गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिं ततोऽक्रूरमुवाच ह ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityājñāpyārthatantrajña āhūya yadupuṅgavamgṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇiṃ tato'krūramuvāca ha
translation: Having thus passed the orders, Kaṃsa who was adept in the technique of achieving his purpose sent for Akrūra, the prominent member of the Yadu clan. Taking his palm in his hand, he spoke to Akrūra.

devanagari: भो भो दानपते मह्यं क्रियतां मैत्रमादृतः । नान्यस्त्वत्तो हिततमो विद्यते भोजवृष्णिषु ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: bho bho dānapate mahyaṃ kriyatāṃ maitramādṛtaḥnānyastvatto hitatamo vidyate bhojavṛṣṇiṣu
translation: “Oh leader of charitable persons![5] Devoted as you are to me, please do a friendly turn to me; for there is nobody else either in my clan of Bhojas or those of Yadus who will guard my interest.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Subodhinī: Akrūra used to give cows as gifts to Brāhmaṇas every day for a period of twelve years and his mother gave food in charity. Hence he is designated as dānapati (the leader of donors).

devanagari: अतस्त्वामाश्रितः सौम्य कार्यगौरवसाधनम् । यथेन्द्रो विष्णुमाश्रित्य स्वार्थमध्यगमद् विभुः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: atastvāmāśritaḥ saumya kāryagauravasādhanamyathendro viṣṇumāśritya svārthamadhyagamad vibhuḥ
translation: Hence, Oh gentle friend, I depend on you as a means of accomplishing an important mission even as the mighty Indra does, depending on god Viṣṇu, achieve his object.

devanagari: गच्छ नन्दव्रजं तत्र सुतौ आवानकदुन्दुभेः । आसाते तौ इहानेन रथेनानय मा चिरम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: gaccha nandavrajaṃ tatra sutau āvānakadundubheḥāsāte tau ihānena rathenānaya mā ciram
translation: Go to the Vraja of Nanda. There dwell two sons of Vasudeva. Please fetch them both here in this chariot. Let there be no delay.

devanagari: निसृष्टः किल मे मृत्युः देवैर्वैकुण्ठसंश्रयैः । तावानय समं गोपैः नन्दाद्यैः साभ्युपायनैः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: nisṛṣṭaḥ kila me mṛtyuḥ devairvaikuṇṭhasaṃśrayaiḥtāvānaya samaṃ gopaiḥ nandādyaiḥ sābhyupāyanaiḥ
translation: It is reported that gods who are dependents of Viṣṇu have ordained my death (at the hands of one of them). Bring both of them along with cowherds headed by Nanda (lest the boys should refuse to come). Instruct Nanda and others to bring the tribute (curds, ghee etc.) so that they would be convinced that the dhanur-makha (sacrifice to Bow) is real and not a ruse to bring them.

devanagari: घातयिष्य इहानीतौ कालकल्पेन हस्तिना । यदि मुक्तौ ततो मल्लैः घातये वैद्युतोपमैः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ghātayiṣya ihānītau kālakalpena hastināyadi muktau tato mallaiḥ ghātaye vaidyutopamaiḥ
translation: When they are brought here, I shall get them killed by an elephant which is like the god of death. If they happen to escape (death from the elephant), I shall get them despatched by two wrestlers who are like thunderbolts.

devanagari: तयोर्निहतयोस्तप्तान् वसुदेवपुरोगमान् । तद्‍बन्धून् निहनिष्यामि वृष्णिभोजदशार्हकान् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tayornihatayostaptān vasudevapurogamāntad‍bandhūn nihaniṣyāmi vṛṣṇibhojadaśārhakān
translation: When both of them are finished, I shall put to death Vasudeva and his brothers who will be grieving (the death of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) and massacre the clans of Vṛṣṇis Bhojas, Dāśārhas. and also my aged father Ugrasena who still dotes for sovereignty and his brother Devaka as well as all other enemies of mine.

devanagari: उग्रसेनं च पितरं स्थविरं राज्यकामुकम् । तद्‍भ्रातरं देवकं च ये चान्ये विद्विषो मम ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: ugrasenaṃ ca pitaraṃ sthaviraṃ rājyakāmukamtad‍bhrātaraṃ devakaṃ ca ye cānye vidviṣo mama
translation: When both of them are finished, I shall put to death Vasudeva and his brothers who will be grieving (the death of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) and massacre the clans of Vṛṣṇis Bhojas, Dāśārhas. and also my aged father Ugrasena who still dotes for sovereignty and his brother Devaka as well as all other enemies of mine.

devanagari: ततश्चैषा मही मित्र भवित्री नष्टकण्टका । जरासन्धो मम गुरुः द्विविदो दयितः सखा ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tataścaiṣā mahī mitra bhavitrī naṣṭakaṇṭakājarāsandho mama guruḥ dvivido dayitaḥ sakhā
translation: It is after that that this earth will be rid of all thorns (free from enemies). My father-in-law Jarāsandha will be like a father unto me and Dvivida (the monkey chief), a dear friend.

devanagari: शम्बरो नरको बाणो मय्येव कृतसौहृदाः । तैरहं सुरपक्षीयान् हत्वा भोक्ष्ये महीं नृपान् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: śambaro narako bāṇo mayyeva kṛtasauhṛdāḥtairahaṃ surapakṣīyān hatvā bhokṣye mahīṃ nṛpān
translation: Śambara, Naraka and Bāṇa have entered into friendly alliance with me. Having killed all the kings siding with gods, I shall enjoy the earth.

devanagari: एतज्ज्ञात्वाऽनय क्षिप्रं रामकृष्णाविहार्भकौ । धनुर्मखनिरीक्षार्थं द्रष्टुं यदुपुरश्रियम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: etajjñātvā'naya kṣipraṃ rāmakṛṣṇāvihārbhakaudhanurmakhanirīkṣārthaṃ draṣṭuṃ yadupuraśriyam
translation: In view of (the knowledge of) these facts, quickly bring the youngsters Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa inducing them to witness the bow-sacrifice as well as the beauty of Mathurā, the capital of Yadus.

devanagari: श्रीअक्रूर उवाच । राजन् मनीषितं सध्र्यक् तव स्वावद्यमार्जनम् । सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समं कुर्याद् दैवं हि फलसाधनम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīakrūra uvācarājan manīṣitaṃ sadhryak tava svāvadyamārjanamsiddhyasiddhyoḥ samaṃ kuryād daivaṃ hi phalasādhanam
translation:
Akrūra replied:
Oh king! What you intend to do is all for the good inasmuch as you try to avert the evil (viz. death). But one should maintain equanimity and try one’s level best whether it results in success or failure. For it is the Providence which is the real cause (instrument) of success.

devanagari: मनोरथान् करोत्युच्चैः जनो दैवहतानपि । युज्यते हर्षशोकाभ्यां तथाप्याज्ञां करोमि ते ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: manorathān karotyuccaiḥ jano daivahatānapiyujyate harṣaśokābhyāṃ tathāpyājñāṃ karomi te
translation: A man builds castles in the air (lit. one entertains very big plans) even though they are thwarted by the Destiny and thus experiences joy and sorrow. I shall, however, carry out your order.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवमादिश्य चाक्रूरं मन्त्रिणश्च विषृज्य सः । प्रविवेश गृहं कंसः तथाक्रूरः स्वमालयम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaevamādiśya cākrūraṃ mantriṇaśca viṣṛjya saḥpraviveśa gṛhaṃ kaṃsaḥ tathākrūraḥ svamālayam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having directed Akrūra (to go to Vraja) and sent away his counsellors, Kaṃsa entered his palace and Akrūra his home.
[back to top]
